CREATE TABLE timestamps (microseconds BIGINT);
INSERT INTO timestamps VALUES (0);
INSERT INTO timestamps VALUES (1667810584123456);
INSERT INTO timestamps VALUES (-1);
INSERT INTO timestamps VALUES (9223372036854775807);
SELECT make_timestamp(microseconds) FROM timestamps WHERE microseconds = 0;
SELECT make_timestamp(microseconds) FROM timestamps WHERE microseconds = 1667810584123456;
SELECT make_timestamp(microseconds) FROM timestamps WHERE microseconds < 0;
SELECT make_timestamp(microseconds) FROM timestamps WHERE microseconds > 9223372036854775806;
SELECT t.microseconds, make_timestamp(t.microseconds) FROM timestamps t WHERE t.microseconds BETWEEN 0 AND 1667810584123456;
CREATE TABLE additional_data (id INT, description VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO additional_data VALUES (1, 'Test Timestamp 0'), (2, 'Test Timestamp Normal');
SELECT a.description, make_timestamp(t.microseconds) FROM additional_data a JOIN timestamps t ON a.id = 1 WHERE t.microseconds = 0;
SELECT a.description, make_timestamp(t.microseconds) FROM additional_data a JOIN timestamps t ON a.id = 2 WHERE t.microseconds = 1667810584123456;
SELECT a.description, make_timestamp(t.microseconds) AS timestamp_output FROM additional_data a JOIN timestamps t ON t.microseconds < 0;
SELECT make_timestamp(t.microseconds) AS timestamp_output FROM timestamps t WHERE microseconds IN (SELECT microseconds FROM timestamps WHERE microseconds > 0);
CREATE VIEW timestamp_view AS SELECT make_timestamp(microseconds) AS my_timestamp FROM timestamps;
SELECT * FROM timestamp_view;
SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_timestamps, MIN(make_timestamp(microseconds)) AS min_timestamp, MAX(make_timestamp(microseconds)) AS max_timestamp FROM timestamps;
SELECT make_timestamp(microseconds) FROM timestamps ORDER BY microseconds DESC LIMIT 3;
